seniority.level: Mid to Senior IC (L4–L6, mapping to ~PM II through Staff PM)
level_signals: 4–10 years of experience in Product Management in the tech industry, with scope appropriate to level (L4: 4–6 yrs, L5: 6–8 yrs, L6: 8–10+ yrs)
Comp range $205,600–$257,000 base consistent with mid-to-senior IC at growth-stage company
Significant autonomy and end-to-end product ownership described

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4–10 years of experience in Product Management in the tech industry, with scope appropriate to level (L4: 4–6 yrs, L5: 6–8 yrs, L6: 8–10+ yrs)
Strong business acumen and analytical rigor, with demonstrated success driving products in ambiguous, high-growth environments
Experience translating complex technical systems into clear product strategies — comfort engaging deeply with engineering and data science teams
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, capable of influencing across technical and non-technical audiences
Experience building products from the ground up and iterating through the scaling journey of a business
Bachelor's or advanced degree in a quantitative, engineering, or related discipline

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in AI/ML, data infrastructure, or marketplace businesses
Strong understanding of the AI landscape — model training workflows, data labeling, evaluation, and deployment
Experience with global payment systems, contributor/gig-economy platforms, or trust & safety domains
Experience working at high-growth startups or scaling consumer/enterprise platforms

scope.surface: Scale GenAI platform — spans demand-side (Task UX, Multi-Dimensional Quality) and supply-side (Pay & Incentives) products powering AI training data and contributor marketplace
scope.business_model: two-sided marketplace (B2B demand side: AI labs and enterprises; supply side: 500,000+ global contributors)
primary_metrics_inferred: ROI / business impact of product initiatives
core_responsibilities: Set the product strategy and roadmap for your area, grounded in customer needs, data analysis, and business impact
Develop and execute a data-driven product roadmap through close collaboration with senior leadership, engineering, operations, data science, analytics, and design
Translate customer and internal-user needs into clear, well-defined functional and technical requirements backed by data analysis and deep understanding of your users
Guide and interface closely with engineering and data teams to define scope, review and refine technical capabilities, prioritize projects for release, and identify new opportunities
Build long-term instrumentation, monitoring, and evaluation capabilities for product performance tracking and insight generation
Establish business cases and projected return on investment to identify and prioritize opportunities
Partner with finance and business leaders to manage impact on the profitability and growth of the overall business
Communicate product vision, strategy, and progress to executive stakeholders and cross-functional partners

compensation.equity_form: equity grant (RSUs/options, subject to Board of Director approval)

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($205,600–$257,000 base) with equity on top
Multiple sub-roles described, giving candidates visibility into product surface options
Clear articulation of product surfaces (Task UX, MDQ, Pay & Incentives) — not vague
Significant autonomy and end-to-end ownership described
Scale operates at the frontier of AI with marquee customers (Meta, Cisco, U.S. government agencies)
Dedicated engineering, design, and data science teams assigned to each PM
red_flags: Role is posted for multiple levels (L4–L6) simultaneously, which may indicate undefined scope or level fitting during process
No explicit mention of visa sponsorship
Location appears onsite-only in SF/NY/Seattle with no remote option stated
90-day waiting period before reconsidering candidates for the same role explicitly noted

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years range and comp; JD names L4/L5/L6 levels but not a single seniority band)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not directly stated; inferred from responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred from JD description of two-sided marketplace)
location.remote_us_eligible (not stated; inferred as false since only SF/NY/Seattle cited for comp and no remote option mentioned)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from statement that range applies to SF/NY/Seattle specifically)
ambiguities: JD covers multiple open PM roles across different sub-teams (Task UX, Multi-Dimensional Quality, Pay & Incentives); it is unclear whether candidates apply to a specific sub-role or are considered for all
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; treated as 'unclear'
Office attendance policy not stated; location type inferred as onsite from compensation geography disclosure but hybrid/remote not explicitly ruled out
